citations.desc: Access and inspection of records and property; submission of itineraries.
citations.narrative:
A responsible adult was not available to accompany APHIS Officials during the inspection process at 1015 hours on April
17, 2026. The inspector attempted to contact the licensee/registrant by calling facility representative. Failure to provide
access to the facility, animals, and records for inspection is a violation of the AWA and regulations. Continued failure to
provide access is serious and may result in enforcement action.

citations.desc: Access and inspection of records and property; submission of itineraries.
citations.narrative:
During the phone call between licensee and APHIS Official, the licensee stated they were in Lawrenceville, GA and had
forgotten to send in the itinerary for their travels.
A written itinerary was not submitted by the licensee prior to exhibition with overnight travel. The licensee was in travel
status at the time of inspection, and no itinerary was available or submitted for overnight travel.
Failure to provide and update itinerary information may result in the inability to conduct an inspection to ensure the
welfare of the animals covered under the Animal Welfare Act.
The licensee must submit a written itinerary to the Deputy Administrator no sooner than 48 hours in advance of any travel
when exhibiting any animal overnight at any location other than the approved home site. The itinerary shall include all
information required in this section, but not limited to, the names, dates, and locations (with addresses) where the animals
will travel, be housed, and be exhibited, including all anticipated dates and locations (with addresses) for any stops and
layovers that allow or require removal of the animals from the transport enclosures.
Corrected at the time of inspection.
End Section

narrative:
2.126(b) 
   
Access and inspection of records and property; submission of itineraries. 
A responsible adult was not available to accompany APHIS Officials during the inspection process at 1015 hours on April 
17, 2026. The inspector attempted to contact the licensee/registrant by calling facility representative. Failure to provide 
access to the facility, animals, and records for inspection is a violation of the AWA and regulations. Continued failure to 
provide access is serious and may result in enforcement action. 
2.126(c) 
   
Access and inspection of records and property; submission of itineraries. 
During the phone call between licensee and APHIS Official, the licensee stated they were in Lawrenceville, GA and had 
forgotten to send in the itinerary for their travels. 
 
A written itinerary was not submitted by the licensee prior to exhibition with overnight travel. The licensee was in travel 
status at the time of inspection, and no itinerary was available or submitted for overnight travel. 
 
Failure to provide and update itinerary information may result in the inability to conduct an inspection to ensure the 
welfare of the animals covered under the Animal Welfare Act. 
 
The licensee must submit a written itinerary to the Deputy Administrator no sooner than 48 hours in advance of any travel 
when exhibiting any animal overnight at any location other than the approved home site. The itinerary shall include all 
information required in this section, but not limited to, the names, dates, and locations (with addresses) where the animals 
will travel, be housed, and be exhibited, including all anticipated dates and locations (with addresses) for any stops and 
layovers that allow or require removal of the animals from the transport enclosures. 
 
Corrected at the time of inspection. 
 
 
End Section
